reasoning: As of early November 2025, gold is trading strongly around $4,000 per ounce, having recently hit a record high of over $4,300 in late October. The target of $3,200 is $800 below the current price, which represents a 20% downward move needed for the market to resolve "No". A 20% drop over just seven weeks is historically an extreme multi-sigma event for gold, which generally exhibits typical annualized volatility around 15%. While there has been an 8% correction from recent peak highs, current macro forecasts from leading banks (e.g. Goldman Sachs, UBS, Morgan Stanley) predict gold finishing the year well above $3,700-$3,800. Absent a historically unprecedented crash in gold prices within roughly 36 trading days, the contract will practically certainly close at or above $3,200. Taking minor structural/tail risks into account, a 96% probability captures this near-certain expectation.
